parameters.description: SI-Unit: m Start-position of the flight leg along y . This parameter is mandatory.

parameters.name: dt_virtual_measurement
parameters.description: SI-Unit: s Temporal interval at which virtual measurements shall be output. It is obligatory to set this parameter. It must not be zero. Therefore, the default value will cause the run to terminate.

parameters.name: dt_virtual_measurement_pr
parameters.description: SI-Unit: s Temporal interval at which virtual profile measurements shall be output. Must not be zero.

parameters.name: dt_virtual_measurement_tr
parameters.description: SI-Unit: s Temporal interval at which virtual trajectory measurements shall be output. Must not be zero.

parameters.name: dt_virtual_measurement_ts
parameters.description: SI-Unit: s Temporal interval at which virtual time series measurements shall be output. Must not be zero.

parameters.name: interpolate_to_location
parameters.description: Flag to switch on/off interpolation onto the exact sampling coordinates. If interpolate_to_location = .T. , the output variables will be bi-linearly interpolated onto the given sampling location, else, a simple nearest neighbor approach is used to sample the variables on the numeric grid. Please note, this feature is only implemented for single-point measurements at the moment, not for trajectory, profile, or multi-point timeseries measurements where off_ts > 0 or off_ts_z > 0 . Furthermore, only atmospheric variables are considered for interpolation, surface or soil variables are not be interpolated and always refer to the grid center. If interpolate_to_location = .F. , all sampled quantities refer to the grid center, also the wind-velocity components, which are linearly interpolated from the staggered grid onto the grid center in this case. To determine the respective grid point, a simple nearest neighbor method is employed.

parameters.name: off_pr
parameters.description: Horizontal sampling radius for profile measurements. The sampling radius is given in grid points horizontally placed around the original measurement location (for profile measurements). The sampling set includes the grid-normal and diagonal grid points. As an example, off_pr = 1 will output 9 profiles (3x3 matrix with the original sampling location in the middle). Note: The value of off_pr must not be larger than the number of ghost points minus one, so for the Wicker-Skamarock advection scheme (see momentum_advec ) 0 <= off_pr <= 2 must hold. The Piascek-Willimas advection scheme only allows off_pr = 0 .

parameters.name: off_pr_z
parameters.description: Vertical sampling radius for profile measurements. Number of grid points along z around the original measurement location (for profile measurements).

parameters.name: off_tr
parameters.description: Horizontal sampling radius for trajectory measurements. The sampling radius is given in grid points horizontally placed around the original measurement location (for trajectory measurements). The sampling set includes the grid-normal and diagonal grid points. As an example, for a grid point on the trajectory, off_tr = 1 will output the original grid point + the 8 horizontally surrounding grid points. Note: The value of off_tr must not be larger than the number of ghost points minus one, so for the Wicker-Skamarock advection scheme (see momentum_advec ) 0 <= off_tr <= 2 must hold. The Piascek-Willimas advection scheme only allows off_tr = 0 .

parameters.name: off_tr_z
parameters.description: Vertical sampling height for trajectory measurements. Number of grid points along z around the original measurement location (for trajectory measurements).

parameters.name: off_ts
parameters.description: Horizontal sampling radius for timeseries measurements. The sampling radius is given in grid points horizontally placed around the original measurement location (for timeseries measurements). The sampling set includes the grid-normal and diagonal grid points. As an example, off_ts = 1 will output 9 timeseries (3x3 matrix with the original sampling location in the middle). Note: The value of off_ts must not be larger than the number of ghost points minus one, so for the Wicker-Skamarock advection scheme (see momentum_advec ) 0 <= off_ts <= 2 must hold. The Piascek-Willimas advection scheme only allows off_ts = 0 .

parameters.name: off_ts_z
parameters.description: Vertical sampling height for timeseries measurements. Number of grid points along z around the original measurement location (for time series measurements).

parameters.name: switch_off_module
parameters.description: Flag to switch on/off module. If switch_off_module = .T. , the virtual measurement module is not activated even if the namelist is present in the parameter file.

parameters.name: vm_time_start
parameters.description: SI-Unit: s Start time of the virtual measurements. vm_time_start refers to the simulation start. Sampling will start at origin_date_time + vm_time_start . Time information given in the virtual-measurement driver file is ignored at the moment.

parameters.name: warn_out_of_domain_locations
parameters.description: Flag to switch on/off warnings for timeseries sampling. If warn_out_of_domain_locations = .T. , warnings will be given when the given sampling location is not within the model domain. Please note, warnings will be only given for point measurements, not for trajectory or profile measurements.

parameters.name: air_density
parameters.description: SI-Unit: kg m -3 Air density used for turbine torque and power calculation.

parameters.name: dt_data_output_wtm
parameters.description: SI-Unit: s Output interval for NetCDF data. Output occurs at the next closest time step after each integer multiple of dt_data_output_wtm . With the default value 0.0 , data will be output every time step.

parameters.name: gear_efficiency
parameters.description: Efficiency of the gear. Default value means an efficiency of 100%.

parameters.name: gear_ratio
parameters.description: Gear ratio from rotor to generator.

parameters.name: generator_efficiency
parameters.description: Electric efficiency of the generator.

parameters.name: generator_inertia
parameters.description: SI-Unit: kg m 2 Moment of inertia of the generator.

parameters.name: generator_power_rated
parameters.description: SI-Unit: W Rated mechanical (rotor) power of the wind turbine.

parameters.name: generator_speed_rated
parameters.description: SI-Unit: rad s -1 Rated generator speed.

parameters.name: generator_torque_max
parameters.description: SI-Unit: N m Maximum Torque of the generator. Should be set to a value 10% above rated.

parameters.name: generator_torque_rate_max
parameters.description: SI-Unit: N m s -1 Maximum generator torque increase rate.

parameters.name: hub_x
parameters.description: SI-Unit: m x-coordinate of the hub. If n_turbines > 1 , values need to be given for each turbine separately. Example for three turbines: hub_x = 1000.0, 1500.0, 2000.0, Note that for turbines which are placed into child domains, the x-coordinate refers to the origin of the child, which is 0.0 at its left boundary.

parameters.name: hub_y
parameters.description: SI-Unit: m y-coordinate of the hub. If n_turbines > 1 , values need to be given for each turbine separately. Example for three turbines: hub_y = 1000.0, 1500.0, 2000.0, Note that for turbines which are placed into child domains, the y-coordinate refers to the origin of the child, which is 0.0 at its left boundary.

parameters.name: hub_z
parameters.description: SI-Unit: m z-coordinate of the hub (hub height). If n_turbines > 1 , values need to be given for each turbine separately. Example for three turbines: hub_z = 150.0, 150.0, 150.0, Note that for turbines which are placed into vertically shifted child domains (see domain_layouts ), the z-coordinate refers to the origin of the root domain, which is 0.0 at its bottom.

parameters.name: n_airfoils
parameters.description: Number of airfoils used in the turbine model. The airfoil data should be provided in the additional input file <run_identifier>_wtm (see here for a file describing the NREL 5 MW turbine).

parameters.name: n_turbines
parameters.description: Number of wind turbines. n_turbines <= 10000 is required.

parameters.name: nacelle_cd
parameters.description: Drag coefficient of the nacelle. nacelle_cd and nacelle_radius both must be non-zero if the nacelle should be included. If n_turbines > 1 , values need to be given for each turbine separately.

parameters.name: nacelle_radius
parameters.description: SI-Unit: m Radius of the nacelle of each turbine. nacelle_radius and nacelle_cd both must be non-zero if the nacelle should be included. A nacelle with a spherical shape and a drag coefficient nacelle_cd is assumed. If n_turbines > 1 , values need to be given for each turbine separately.

parameters.name: pitch_angle
parameters.description: SI-Unit: degree Constant pitch angle of the blades that is used if pitch_control = .F. . If n_turbines > 1 , values need to be given for each turbine separately.

parameters.name: pitch_control
parameters.description: Parameter to switch on the pitch control. This parameter controls the pitch angle such that constant power is produced for wind speeds greater than the rated wind speed. The parameter generator_power_rated is used here.

parameters.name: pitch_rate
parameters.description: SI-Unit: degree s -1 Maximum pitch rate.

parameters.name: region_15_min
parameters.description: SI-Unit: rad s -1 Lower generator speed boundary of region 1.5. Region 1.5 is a start-up region between regions 1 and 2 that represents the lower limit of the generator speed, which limits the operational speed range of the wind turbine. Note, region 1 is before cut-in wind speed. Here, wind is used to accelerate the rotor so no power is produced. See Jonkman et al., 2009 for more details.

parameters.name: region_2_min
parameters.description: SI-Unit: rad s -1 Lower generator speed boundary of region 2.

parameters.name: region_2_slope
parameters.description: SI-Unit: N m (rad s -1 ) -2 Slope constant for region 2. In region 2, a constant tip speed ratio is maintained to optimize the power capture. In this region, the generator torque is proportional to the square of the filtered generator speed. See Jonkman et al., 2009 for more details.

parameters.name: rotor_inertia
parameters.description: SI-Unit: kg m 2 Moment of inertia of the rotor.

parameters.name: rotor_radius
parameters.description: SI-Unit: m Radius of the rotor. If n_turbines > 1 , values need to be given for each turbine separately.

parameters.name: rotor_speed
parameters.description: SI-Unit: rad s -1 Initial rotational speed for each turbine. If n_turbines > 1 , values need to be given for each turbine separately.

parameters.name: segment_length_tangential
parameters.description: Tangential length of rotor segments. The segment length is given as factor of the minimum grid spacing MIN( dx , dy , dz ).

parameters.name: segment_width_radial
parameters.description: Radial width of rotor segments. The segment width is given as factor of the minimum grid spacing MIN( dx , dy , dz ).

parameters.name: smearing_kernel_size
parameters.description: Size of the smearing kernel given as multiples of the grid spacing dx . For details, refer to ​https://palm.muk.uni-hannover.de/trac/wiki/doc/tec/wtm . A large smearing kernel results in an overestimation of tip speed ratio and power output. For coarse grid spacings (e.g., 12 grid points per rotor diameter) the power overestimation can be as large as 25%. This error can be reduced by setting smearing_kernel_size = 1.0 . Do not use values below 1.0 because then the number of grid cells inside the smearing kernel becomes very small (<10). In this case the sum of the body forces acting on the flow may be lower than the force acting on the rotor blade.

parameters.name: speed_control
parameters.description: Parameter to switch on the speed control. This parameter controls the rotational speed of the turbine for wind speeds less than rated wind speed.

parameters.name: switch_off_module
parameters.description: Parameter to switch off the entire wind turbine module. switch_off_module = .T. can be used to switch off this module without removing (or commenting) the namelist from the namelist file.

parameters.name: tilt_angle
parameters.description: SI-Unit: degree Tilt angle of the rotor plane. A positive angle means the bottom of the rotor plane is tilted away from the tower to avoid the blades hitting the tower.

parameters.name: time_turbine_on
parameters.description: SI-Unit: s Simulation time at which the turbines are switched on.

parameters.name: tip_loss_correction
parameters.description: Parameter to switch on the tip loss correction.

parameters.name: tower_cd
parameters.description: Drag coefficient of the tower. If n_turbines > 1 , values need to be given for each turbine separately.

parameters.name: tower_diameter
parameters.description: SI-Unit: m Diameter of the cylindrical tower of the wind turbine. This parameter must be non-zero if a tower should be included. The parameter must be specified for every turbine separately.

parameters.name: yaw_angle
parameters.description: SI-Unit: degree Yaw angle for each turbine. A positive yaw means a rotation of the rotor in clockwise direction within the xy-plane. At a yaw angle of zero degrees the rotor is facing west.

parameters.name: yaw_control
parameters.description: Parameter to switch on the yaw controller. If yaw_control = .F. , then the constant yaw angle specified by yaw_angle will be used. If set yaw_control = .T., then the yaw angle will adjust according to the local wind direction, as soon as the yaw misalignment reaches yaw_misalignment_max until yaw_misalignment_min is reached. The rotational speed of the yaw mechanism is controlled by yaw_speed .

parameters.name: yaw_misalignment_max
parameters.description: SI-Unit: rad Maximum tolerated yaw misalignment.

parameters.name: yaw_misalignment_min
parameters.description: SI-Unit: rad Minimum yaw misalignment for which the actuator stops.

parameters.name: yaw_speed
parameters.description: SI-Unit: rad s -1 Speed of the yaw actuator.
